title: UIAO Adapter Registry Schema
description: JSON Schema for UIAO adapter registry entries. Adapters are registered along two orthogonal axes: the operational class (`class`) describes how the adapter behaves at runtime (change-making vs read-only assessing), and the mission class (`mission-class`) maps the adapter to the canonical UIAO_003 Adapter Segmentation Overview doctrinal roles against SSOT. An adapter declares one value on each axis. See ARCHITECTURE.md §3.2 for the dual-axis rationale.

schema-version.description: Semantic version of this registry document schema.

registry-class.description: Declares which registry file this is. Each registry file pins to one operational class so that canon review and CI drift-scan can treat modernization and conformance adapters independently.

updated.description: ISO 8601 date of the most recent registry update.

adapters.description: Ordered list of adapter entries registered in this file.

id.description: Kebab-case stable identifier. Never renamed once assigned; retire via status=DEPRECATED + successor field instead.

class.description: Operational axis — how the adapter behaves at runtime. `modernization` = change-making adapter (writes to target environment). `conformance` = read-only assessor (observes state, never mutates target).

mission-class.description: Doctrinal axis — canonical UIAO_003 Adapter Segmentation Overview class. `identity|telemetry|policy|enforcement|integration` are the five named classes in UIAO_003 §4.2–§4.7. `integration` (added per resolved ODA-15, 2026-04-15) covers change-making actions against a target environment (create/update/delete in the authoritative system) — e.g., identity provisioning, tenant configuration, network enforcement-point updates, ITSM ticketing, baseline application. Use `unmapped` ONLY when no current doctrinal class cleanly covers the adapter's role; such cases MUST also appear as an open decision in ARCHITECTURE.md §13. Use `mixed` when an adapter straddles two canonical roles (requires `mission-class-notes`).

mission-class-notes.description: Required when mission-class is `mixed` or `unmapped`; free text explaining the gap or straddle, with reference to the open-decision ID in ARCHITECTURE.md §13 when applicable.

status.description: Lifecycle state. `reserved` = slot name claimed but no implementation yet. `proposed` = design drafted, not yet approved. `active` = in production use. `deprecated` = being retired; must name successor. `retired` = no longer in use.

successor.description: Required when status=deprecated or status=retired. Kebab-case id of the replacing adapter.

phase.description: Deployment phase marker aligned with CONMON.md §8 runner strategy (Phase 1: GitHub-hosted windows-latest; Phase 2: Azure Government self-hosted; Phase 3+: production rollout).

vendor.description: Originating vendor, agency, or project (e.g., Microsoft, Palo Alto Networks, CISA).

license.description: SPDX license identifier or proprietary designation.

runtime-version.description: Pinned runtime or tool version for reproducible runs.

runner-class.description: Execution environment for the adapter's workflow. See CONMON.md §8.

policy-engine.description: Policy evaluation engine if applicable (e.g., OPA/Rego, custom, none).

policy-pin.description: Pinned policy baseline version or commit SHA.

scope.description: Target surfaces or subsystems the adapter operates against.

outputs.description: Output artifact types produced by the adapter (e.g., findings.json, metadata.json, POA&M row, SCR snapshot).

triggers.description: How the adapter is invoked (e.g., workflow_dispatch, repository_dispatch, schedule, SCR-pre-snapshot, SCR-post-snapshot).

evidence-class.description: Evidence classification per CONMON.md §4 (baseline, interval, SCR, incident, annual).

retention-years.description: Minimum evidence retention in years per FedRAMP ConMon Playbook §7 and agency policy.

freshness-window-hours.description: Maximum age in hours before evidence from this adapter is treated as stale by the DRIFT-SEMANTIC freshness evaluator (UIAO_016 §4). Evidence older than this window emits a DRIFT-SEMANTIC finding at P2. Absent value falls back to the evaluator's control-family default (e.g., 30 days for AC, 7 days for AU/SI). Per-adapter values let high-signal feeds (identity, security alerts) demand tighter windows than background adapters (vulnerability scans, patch state).

controls.description: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 control identifiers this adapter supports (e.g., CA-5, CM-8, RA-5).

automation-domain.description: NIST SP 800-137 Appendix D security automation domain(s). See ARCHITECTURE.md §15.2 for the canonical list of 11.

gcc-boundary.description: Sanctioned governance boundary. UIAO canon: GCC-Moderate only. Amazon Connect Contact Center in Commercial Cloud is the sole named exception.

ssot-mutation.description: Declares whether the adapter mutates SSOT. Canon invariant: no adapter mutates SSOT. Only `never` is permitted; the field exists to make the invariant auditable.

certificate-anchored.description: Canon invariant: every adapter transaction carries certificate-anchored provenance. Must be true.

trust-anchor.description: UIAO_110 DRIFT-IDENTITY runtime issuer-chain anchor. Required when certificate-anchored=true; consumed by uiao.governance.issuer_resolution.IssuerResolver. Either subject DN or SHA-256 fingerprint is required; both may be present (fingerprint preferred under cross-signing). A bare string is interpreted as the subject DN.

subject.description: Trust-anchor subject DN, e.g. "CN=Microsoft RSA Root Certificate Authority 2017".

fingerprint_sha256.description: Anchor's SHA-256 fingerprint, lowercase hex, no separators (64 chars).

ztmm-pillars.description: UIAO_120 / §3.6 ZTMM Integration. Lists the CISA Zero Trust Maturity Model v2.0 pillar(s) this adapter's evidence outputs inform. Consumed by uiao.governance.ztmm.ZTMMScoreCalculator. Empty list permitted (read-only / informational adapters).

object-identity-only.description: Canon invariant: object identity only; no person identity. Must be true.

references.description: Canonical references (document IDs, URLs, ADRs) that authorize this adapter entry.
